message:
fix(storage): CTE-wrap hot-path SELECTs to bypass fsqlite prepared-stmt fast path (#254, #255)

Context
-------
Under concurrent write load, fsqlite's `ad_hoc_query_supports_prepared_reuse`
routes plain single-table lookups like `SELECT … FROM issues WHERE id = ?`
through a prepared-statement fast-path cache keyed by plan, not by the
underlying B-tree state. Stale cache hits have been observed returning
zero rows for freshly-inserted IDs, returning the wrong assignee for a
just-updated claim, and aliasing a live bead onto a tombstoned one on
the ID resolver's hot path.

The earlier #229 workaround (switching from `query_row_with_params` to
`query_with_params`) did NOT escape the fast path — both helpers route
through the same predicate. The only shape fsqlite treats as "must
dispatch through the full planner" is one where `select.with.is_some()`
is true. A trivial CTE wrapper with a one-row `target(id_value)` is
enough to flip the predicate and force the uncached B-tree walk.

Changes (src/storage/sqlite.rs)
-------------------------------
Wrap the following single-row, hot-path lookups in a `WITH target(id_value)
AS (SELECT ?)` CTE:

- `SqliteStorage::create_issue`'s duplicate-id pre-check.
- `claim_with_update`'s pre-update assignee TOCTOU guard (ran inside
  the CONCURRENT transaction — a stale cache hit here defeats the claim
  atomicity guarantee entirely).
- `claim_with_update`'s post-update assignee read when 0 rows were
  updated (used to produce a precise "already claimed" error; a stale
  read here silently converted a claim loss into "issue not found").
- `issue_status_in_tx` (pre-update dependency-target validation — a
  stale hit was silently accepting tombstoned targets).
- `id_exists` (general existence check).
- `is_tombstone` (on the resolver's hot path via
  `IdResolver::resolve_fallible`; this is the path that was observed
  aliasing live beads onto tombstoned ones).

Also in `get_issue_from_conn`, add a defensive invariant: if the row
returned ever has a different `id` than the one requested, return a
loud `storage consistency` error instead of silently surfacing a
foreign issue through `br show` / `br update` (#255).

Additional fsqlite workarounds in the same sweep
------------------------------------------------
- `upsert_metadata_key_in_tx`: replace DELETE+INSERT with UPDATE-then-
  INSERT-if-zero-rows-affected. The old pattern caused per-sync
  freelist churn on the metadata table that compounded with the
  freelist bugs the rebuild path fixes separately address.
- `insert_default_metadata`: switch the correlated
  `INSERT ... WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 ...)` to the clearer
  `INSERT OR IGNORE`. Same end state, one round trip, no subquery.
- `get_blocks_dep_edges` and the dependency-graph builder in the
  cycle-detection path: split their `SELECT ... UNION SELECT ...`
  queries into two independent `conn.query(...)` calls and merge in
  Rust. fsqlite's planner mishandled the UNION shape with these
  prepared statements, producing empty edge lists for the parent-child
  half of the union under certain transaction states. Functionally
  identical, but no UNION means no planner surprise.

doctor write-probe (src/cli/commands/doctor.rs)
-----------------------------------------------
Apply the same CTE-wrap to `write_probe_after_repair`'s read-back query
so the post-repair health probe cannot itself be fooled by a stale
prepared-statement cache and report a DB as "healthy" when it is not.

No schema changes.  No behavior change on a cold cache or when the
planner already dispatches a query through the slow path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
